identity.name: Prisma Browser — Drive-by Download from Phishing Page (T1189)
identity.summary: A managed user lands on a phishing page whose JavaScript triggers an unsolicited binary download with no user gesture. Prisma Browser observes the auto-initiated download, flags the executable MIME type from a non-SaaS origin, submits the artifact to WildFire, and the NGFW EAL file-type policy independently matches the egress — a stitched drive-by compromise detection.
identity.description: Drive-by compromise turns a single page visit into code on disk. In this scenario the cortex-browser-attacker EAL plugin drives the managed Prisma Browser to a phishing-tagged origin (`phish.cortexsim-test.invalid/q3-earnings-update`) whose page script auto-triggers a binary download with no click. Prisma Browser sees the download was initiated programmatically (no user gesture), records the file name, SHA-256, and `application/x-msdownload` MIME from a non-SaaS origin, and submits the artifact to WildFire. In parallel the NGFW network EAL matches its file-type download policy against the same egress, giving NDR-plane corroboration. Detection layers: a BIOC on gesture-less auto-download; analytics on executable MIME from a non-sanctioned origin; an NDR analytic on the NGFW file-type policy hit; and a WildFire-submission delivery check. A correlation rule fuses the browser auto-download BIOC with the executable-MIME analytic into one initial-access incident.

prerequisites.description: Managed Prisma Browser deployed and reporting telemetry to XSIAM; WildFire integration enabled.
Lab environment matching scenario SIM-BROWSER-002 (cortex-browser-attacker installed; NGFW EAL collector reachable).

payload.code:
# Drives the managed Prisma Browser through the drive-by-download campaign.
python3 -m scripts.eal_simulator.cli run /tmp/sim-browser-002-eal.yml --live
# Campaign: navigate https://phish.cortexsim-test.invalid/q3-earnings-update
#           download (JS auto-trigger, no user gesture) -> WildFire submission

expected_artifacts.description: Download action initiated without a user gesture from a phishing-tagged origin.
expected_artifacts.expected_value: action = "download" and user_gesture = false and origin = "phish.cortexsim-test.invalid"

expected_artifacts.description: Downloaded artifact with an executable MIME type.
Outbound file download seen by the NGFW EAL file-type policy.

biocs.name: Prisma Browser — auto-download initiated by JS without user gesture
biocs.description: Prisma Browser — auto-download initiated by JS without user gesture. A file download begins programmatically (no click / no user gesture) from a page the managed browser rendered.
biocs.logic:
dataset = prisma_browser_events
| filter action = "download"
| filter user_gesture = false
| filter file_name != null
| filter origin not in ("drive.google.com", "onedrive.live.com", "box.com")

xql_queries.name: Pre-flight: plugin metadata visible to operator
xql_queries.query:
dataset = prisma_browser_events
| filter _time > to_timestamp(current_time() - 1800)
| filter campaign_id = "BC-BROWSER-002"
| filter action in ("navigate", "download")
| fields _time, campaign_id, action, origin, url, file_name
| sort asc _time

xql_queries.name: Prisma Browser — downloaded artifact MIME application/x-msdownload from non-SaaS origin
xql_queries.query:
dataset = prisma_browser_events
| filter _time > to_timestamp(current_time() - 1800)
| filter action = "download"
| filter file_mime_type in ("application/x-msdownload", "application/x-dosexec", "application/octet-stream")
| filter origin not in ("drive.google.com", "onedrive.live.com", "box.com")
| fields _time, user, origin, url, file_name, file_mime_type, file_sha256
| sort desc _time

xql_queries.name: NGFW EAL — file-type download policy matches downloaded artifact
xql_queries.query:
dataset = panw_ngfw_threat_raw
| filter _time > to_timestamp(current_time() - 1800)
| filter log_subtype = "file" or sub_type = "data"
| filter file_type in ("PE", "msdownload", "dll")
| filter dst_host = "phish.cortexsim-test.invalid" or url contains "q3-earnings-update"
| fields _time, src_ip, dst_host, app, file_name, file_type, action
| sort desc _time

xql_queries.name: PB → WildFire submission within download-policy MTTD
xql_queries.query:
dataset = prisma_browser_events
| filter _time > to_timestamp(current_time() - 1800)
| filter action = "download" and wildfire_submitted = true
| fields _time, user, origin, file_name, file_sha256, wildfire_verdict
| sort desc _time

correlation_rules.name: Browser Drive-by Download — Gesture-less Executable
correlation_rules.logic: BIOC(Prisma Browser — auto-download initiated by JS without user gesture)

analytics_modules: Cortex XSIAM Analytics — Executable MIME from non-SaaS origin

products.evidence_query: dataset = prisma_browser_events | filter action = "download" and user_gesture = false | fields _time, user, origin, file_name
WildFire verdict on the auto-downloaded executable artifact.
Correlation CR-BROWSER-0002 raises an initial-access incident on a gesture-less executable download.

use_cases.name: Browser-Initiated Malware Download
use_cases.description: Detect and block drive-by binary downloads triggered by phishing pages inside the managed Prisma Browser, with WildFire and NGFW corroboration.

test_cases.objective: Gesture-less executable download fires the Prisma Browser BIOC and submits to WildFire.
preconditions: Managed Prisma Browser reporting to XSIAM.
WildFire integration enabled; PB download-control rule imported.
steps: Observe the download alert and WildFire verdict in the Cortex console.
success_criteria: BIOC `Prisma Browser — auto-download initiated by JS without user gesture` fires within 60s.
Executable-MIME and WildFire XQL queries return >= 1 row.
NGFW file-type policy XQL returns >= 1 row when NGFW EAL is inline.

test_cases.objective: Browser + network signals stitch into one initial-access incident.

preventive_controls: Enable Prisma Browser download control to block gesture-less downloads of executable MIME types.
Force WildFire inspection on all browser-sourced downloads before they reach disk.
Apply NGFW file-blocking policy for PE/DLL/script types from uncategorized or phishing-tagged origins.
detection_engineering: Alert on any download with user_gesture=false delivering an executable MIME from a non-SaaS origin.
Correlate the browser download event with the NGFW file-type policy hit on the same artifact.
Hunt for downloads whose SHA-256 returns a malicious or unknown WildFire verdict.
